methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Avalanche Energy is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Robin Langtry that has raised $80M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of four private peers at the same stage and sector (Watershed, Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy), alongside one public comparable, reflecting the frontier-tech nature of the climate and energy sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies a $160M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the four same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a $175M valuation, with a range of $110M to $1.8B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in the peer set, notably Watershed's $1.8B valuation as an outlier relative to the tighter cluster of $110M–$200M among the other three peers.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $136M to $201M, with a median of $168M. This modest spread between the two methods (both convergent near $160–$175M) suggests reasonable directional alignment. Key uncertainties include the relevance of Watershed as a true comparable given its substantially higher valuation, and the limited granularity on peer-specific capital efficiency metrics or technology differentiation that might better explain the $110M–$200M peer spread.
